name: FeatureCardTwentyThree
description: Feature section with separate media and card layout, featuring tags and arrow navigation.
details: Use for feature listings with prominent imagery. Layout: CardStackTextBox header → Grid of feature items. Each item has separate media (aspect-square) above a card containing title, tags row, and arrow icon. Media and card are visually distinct with gap-6 spacing. Tags use the Tag component and arrow rotates -45deg on hover. Best for visual-focused feature listings with clean separation between imagery and content.

description.example: Stay updated with the latest trends and insights

id.note: Unique identifier for the feature item

title.example: Discover the essential features of our app

tags.note: Array of tag strings displayed using Tag component

imageSrc.note: Either imageSrc or videoSrc should be provided

videoSrc.note: Either imageSrc or videoSrc should be provided

imageAlt.note: Falls back to feature title if not provided

videoAriaLabel.note: Falls back to feature title if not provided

propsSchema.features: Array<{ id: string, title: string, tags: string[], imageSrc?: string, videoSrc?: string, imageAlt?: string, videoAriaLabel?: string, onFeatureClick?: () => void }>
propsSchema.title: string
propsSchema.titleSegments?: Array<{ type: 'text', content: string } | { type: 'image', src: string, alt?: string }> - For inline images in title
propsSchema.buttons?: Array<{text: string, onClick?: () => void, href?: string}>

usageExample: <FeatureCardTwentyThree features={[{ id: '1', title: 'Discover the essential features of our app', tags: ['Features', 'Jan 29, 2025'], imageSrc: '/feature1.jpg' }, { id: '2', title: 'How to organize your tasks effectively', tags: ['Guides', 'Feb 03, 2025'], imageSrc: '/feature2.jpg' }]} animationType="slide-up" title="Our Features" description="Stay updated with the latest trends and insights" textboxLayout="default" useInvertedBackground={false} />
do: Best for feature showcases with clean separation between imagery and content
dont: Do not use for text-heavy features
